action: Add compact RFC 9162 consistency proofs between peer-trigger Merkle checkpoints.

progress.finding: Append-only relation between an earlier and later checkpoint can be verified with a minimal logarithmic consistency path rather than replaying the complete earlier prefix.
progress.verification: The exact committed module was re-fetched. Byte-equivalent algorithm logic passed 136 prefix transitions for all 0 < first < second <= 17 with proof-size bounds. Provider CI for the committed adversarial suite was not observed.
progress.limits: Proof validity does not authenticate roots, prevent split-view equivocation without witness exchange, prove payload truth, or provide hash-algorithm agility.

handoff: A future Frontier slice should bind checkpoint exchange to signatures or independent witnesses and add equivocation comparison across observers. Coordination writers may replace full-prefix append-only checks with this compact proof only after exact-commit tests execute successfully in CI.
